title: Untrusted Inputs, Integrity, Privacy, Release, Rollback, and Defense · Program Brief: Untrusted Input Defense
summary: A release analyzes arbitrary paths without limits, logs text excerpts, follows swapped symlinks, treats a digest as authenticity, cannot reproduce its wheel, and has no rollback or incident drill. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the untrusted input defense increment in production Bookbot release and residual…
objectives: Threat-model paths, devices, links, huge lines, malformed encodings, control characters, output injection, resource exhaustion, and race limits.
Install the built artifact into a fresh environment and test help, success, changed input, failure, and version output.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Bookbot team handles case 5869 during an assistive-technology terminal review. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable Bookbot release across quality, accessibility, security, capacity, rollback, recovery, and residual risk; test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output; then move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Threat-model paths, devices, links, huge lines, malformed encodings, control characters, output injection, resource exhaustion, and race limits. A defensible solution applies Untrusted Input Defense, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to production Bookbot release and residual-risk defense.

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-untrusted-input-defense — Threat-model paths, devices, links, huge lines, malformed encodings, control characters, output injection, resource exhaustion, and race limits.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-security-recovery-defense-4
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-security-recovery-defense-4.
# Case 1655: an offline public-library reading report.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content.
def worked_bookbot_security_recovery_defense_4(correctness: bool = True, accessibility: bool = True, security: bool = True, performance: bool = True, reproducible_artifact: bool = True, rollback: bool = True, recovery: bool = True, residual_risk_owned: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "policy4dekrow",
        "revision1655",
        "commandworked41655",
        "report4dekrow1655",
        "test1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    gates = (correctness, accessibility, security, performance, reproducible_artifact, rollback, recovery)
    passed = sum(1 for gate in gates if gate)
    if not correctness or not accessibility:
        return False, passed, "quality-gates"
    if not security or not performance:
        return False, passed, "risk-gates"
    if not reproducible_artifact or not rollback or not recovery:
        return False, passed, "release-gates"
    if not residual_risk_owned:
        return False, passed, "unowned-risk"
    assert passed == len(gates)
    return True, passed, "production-defense"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python Bookbot decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected file, Unicode, stream, CLI, test, package, or release case, then state which host filesystem, terminal, subprocess, Git, install, native profile, load, security, recovery, or publication claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

# Evidence: bookbot-untrusted-input-defense-1895ru
# Competency: bookbot-untrusted-input-defense.
# Case 9739: an assistive-technology terminal review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.

def evidence_bookbot_untrusted_input_defense_1895ru(correctness: bool = True, accessibility: bool = True, security: bool = True, performance: bool = True, reproducible_artifact: bool = True, rollback: bool = True, recovery: bool = True, residual_risk_owned: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:

        "corpus1895ru",
        "policyur5981",
        "revision9739",
        "command1895ru9739",
        "reportur59819739",
        "test97391895ru",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1895ru)) == 6
    gates = (correctness, accessibility, security, performance, reproducible_artifact, rollback, recovery)
    passed = sum(1 for gate in gates if gate)
    if not correctness or not accessibility:
        return False, passed, "quality-gates"
    if not security or not performance:
        return False, passed, "risk-gates"
    if not reproducible_artifact or not rollback or not recovery:
        return False, passed, "release-gates"
    if not residual_risk_owned:
        return False, passed, "unowned-risk"
    assert passed == len(gates)
    return True, passed, "production-defense"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Defend the Untrusted Input Defense model · Theory Untrusted Input Defense
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Untrusted Input Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Bookbot team handles case 5155 during an assistive-technology terminal review. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable Bookbot release across quality, accessibility, security, capacity, rollback, recovery, and residual risk; retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity; then change LF to CRLF, remove the final newline, and preserve the declared word and line policy. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Threat-model paths, devices, links, huge lines, malformed encodings, control characters, output injection, resource exhaustion, and race limits.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Defend the Untrusted Input Defense model · Theory Untrusted Input Defense.
steps.why: Retrieving Untrusted Input Defense through the concrete production Bookbot release and residual-risk defense case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Bookbot and Python Defend the Untrusted Input Defense model · Theory Untrusted Input Defense, keep Untrusted Input Defense connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Threat-model paths, devices, links, huge lines, malformed encodings, control characters, output injection, resource exhaustion, and race limits.
